content:
SYSTEM: Follow Arpent's loaded operating contract and inspect only authoritative context.
USER: Read Arpent globally and explain its architecture, operating modes, source-of-truth boundaries, and memory boundary before recommending where to start.

text: Arpent is filesystem-native and keeps Markdown as its durable source of truth.
Minimal mode uses direct-file operation; full mode uses CLI-mediated operation.

Arpent is a filesystem-native continuity layer: canonical Markdown and original attachments are the durable source of truth, while indexes and most databases are rebuildable or coordinated derivatives. Minimal uses direct-file operation on the same typed vault; full uses CLI-mediated locking, routing, transactions, search, todo state, import, and recovery. The agent layer interprets intent but does not own the data. Optional external memory has a separate provider opt-in boundary and is not automatic recall infrastructure. Start with the compact skill and vault marker for ordinary work, then load only the operation-specific contract; use the complete reference index plus implementation when changing Arpent itself.
